prerequisites: GitHub account; org apps require permission on the organization

mechanisms.description: GitHub App Manifest flow: a local server form-POSTs a declarative manifest to github.com, the user approves in the browser, and the redirect code is exchanged UNauthenticated for the full credential set. The code IS the credential — no pre-existing token anywhere. The only provider in the corpus besides SoundCloud where a stranger can start from zero.

methods.notes: The manifest must be a form-POST body field, so a helper serves a local auto-submitting HTML form; the conversion exchange requires no authentication. The one-time code is valid for one hour.

credentials.rotation: Generate a new private key from the app settings page; old keys can be revoked.

scopes.notes: Declared as default_permissions + default_events in the manifest; installation selects repositories.

gaps: App names must be globally unique and a collision fails at the browser form — no programmatic pre-check.
The browser approval step is inherently interactive; there is no headless path (by design).
The manifest browser flow is not yet executable by the generic engine (mechanism type: manifest).
